behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is at real `git` rename detection plus CLI parsing/aggregation. A unit test of categorization logic can miss real failures where `git diff/status` emits rename data differently (or not at all) under real repo conditions; mocking can hide that.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temporary real git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert report shows 1 rename and 0 add/0 delete for that change.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often comes from subprocess output encoding/byte handling and actual git binary diff behavior. Mocked text fixtures can pass while real binary content causes decode/parsing crashes.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, commit a binary file (e.g., random bytes), modify it, run the CLI process, assert exit code is success and binary change is counted in the report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real git semantics in an unborn-HEAD repository. Mocking command responses can hide true exit codes/stderr patterns that trigger crashes.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI as a subprocess, assert successful exit and an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against real repository history and git command behavior across multiple change types. Module-level tests won’t reliably catch mismatches caused by real command invocation, revision selection, or parsing of real outputs.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a scripted history containing known adds/deletes/renames/modifies over several commits; run CLI and assert reported counts exactly match expected totals.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/working-tree behaviors only reproduced faithfully with real git operations. Mocking file-status APIs can mask real discrepancies in command flags or revision targets.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, make identical change sets in two runs (one staged, one unstaged), invoke CLI each time, and assert normalized structured outputs are identical.
